title: Lynaliskar K'ra Snyffulnimatta
content:

{{Quote|I hate it when you call me that…|"Sniffles"|<ref name="DT1" />}}
'''Lynaliskar K'ra Snyffulnimatta''', also known simply as '''Sniffles''', was an [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as the [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] on board the [[Maka-Eekai L4000 transport|vessel]] ''[[Uhumele]]'', guiding the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] alongside the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Crys Taanzer]]. He and the rest of the crew were grounded by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial forces]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] shortly after the [[Declaration of a New Order|declaration]] of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. After the ''Uhumele'' took aboard two [[Outlaw|fugitives]] seeking to escape the Empire—the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dass Jennir]] and [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] [[New Plympto resistance (Imperial Period)|resistance fighter]] [[Bomo Greenbark]]—the crew put together a plan to escape with the pair's help, and Snyffulnimatta successfully navigated the ship off-world when the plan came to fruition.

After their escape, Snyffulnimatta and the rest of the crew agreed to help Greenbark rescue his [[Mesa Greenbark|wife]] and [[Resa Greenbark|daughter]], who had been captured and sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. The group traveled first to the slave world of [[Orvax IV]], where they learned of the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Greenbark's wife and the location of his daughter on the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], to which they next traveled. On Esseles, the group [[Assault on Dezono Qua's villa|attacked]] the [[Qua family's villa|villa]] belonging to [[Dezono Qua]], the man who had bought Greenbark's daughter, only to discover she too was dead. After Qua was killed, Greenbark revealed to the group that Jennir was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], and Snyffulnimatta was amongst those in the ship's crew who believed that having Jedi with them would be a bad idea. The group eventually agreed to continue without the Jedi.

The crew of the ''Uhumele'' later decided to sell [[Dreypa's Oubliette]], a valuable piece of cargo that they possessed. The buyer for the cargo was [[Haka Hai]], an old acquaintance of the ''Uhumele''{{'s}} [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Schurk-Heren]], who operated in the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] of the world of [[Mimban/Legends|Mimban]]. Upon meeting with Hai, though, the group became involved in a [[Lightfight|firefight]] when Hai betrayed them and attempted to steal the cargo. [[Lumbra|One]] of Hai's men then betrayed him in an attempt to claim the [[Oubliette (stasis casket)|Oubliette]]. During the [[Skirmish on Mimban|fight]], Snyffulnimatta was killed while trying to save Taanzer, jumping between her and an incoming [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt.

==Biography==
===Trapped on New Plympto===
{{Quote|The crew of the ''Valance'' would spill their guts to the Imps just to put themselves in the clear.|Snyffulnimatta describes the ''Valance''{{'s}} crew|<ref name="DT2">[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:SnifflesandCrys.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Snyffulnimatta and his crewmate Crys Taanzer]]
By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Lynaliskar K'ra Snyffulnimatta had come to serve as the [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] on board the [[Maka-Eekai L4000 transport]] ''[[Uhumele]]''. On the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], he served under the [[Yarkora/Legends|Yarkora]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Schurk-Heren]] beside the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Crys Taanzer]], who [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the ship and gave the Elomin the nickname "Sniffles." Snyffulnimatta and Taanzer worked together to keep the ship on [[Time/Legends|time]] during its regular [[Transport/Legends|transport]] runs.<ref name="CSWE" /> One [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|Declaration]] of a [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]—the [[Politics/Legends|political]] regime of the newly formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—the ''Uhumele'' and its crew were visiting the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]], landing their ship in a [[Cadgel Meadows spaceport|spaceport]] built in an area known as [[Cadgel Meadows]]. While the vessel was in the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], all ships present were grounded by Imperial [[Imperial Army/Legends|forces]] stationed in the area, who were engaged in a [[Cleansing of New Plympto|conflict]] with the native [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] population.<ref name="DT1" /> Schurk-Heren's crew wished to avoid Imperials searching their ship at all costs, but while trapped, Snyffulnimatta and the rest of the crew came to mistrust the crew of another grounded ship, the ''[[Valance]]'', whom the navigator believed would inform the Imperials if any other ship attempted to leave Cadgel Meadows.<ref name="DT2" />

One night during the grounding, the ''Uhumele'' was boarded by a pair of survivors from the [[New Plympto resistance (Imperial Period)|New Plympto resistance]] who had escaped from a recent [[Battle of Half-Axe Pass|battle]] in an area known as [[Half-Axe Pass]]. The pair, seeking to avoid Imperial attention, jumped upon Taanzer after seeing her standing in the open hatch of the ''Uhumele'', hoping to prevent her from calling out. However as soon as the pair entered the ship, the rest of the crew, including Snyffulnimatta, held the boarders at [[Blaster/Legends|blasterpoint]], causing them to release the pilot, who punched one of the boarders before the Elomin pulled her away. Schurk-Heren then introduced the crew, identifying Snyffulnimatta by his nickname, much to the Elomin's annoyance, and explained to the boarders that they too wanted to avoid Imperial attention. The boarders then identified themselves, the Human as [[Dass Jennir]] and the Nosaurian as [[Bomo Greenbark]], and explained that they had come to the spaceport seeking the latter's family, who had evacuated to the area during the recent battle. Snyffulnimatta, however, chose to leave with Taanzer at this point instead of remaining for further introductions.<ref name="DT1" />

===Hunt for the Greenbarks===
{{Quote|Jennir had better hurry—he's going to miss out.|Snyffulnimatta, enjoying a meal on Esseles|<ref name="DT5">[[Dark Times 5|''Dark Times'' 5]]</ref>}}
Jennir and Greenbark were allowed to stay on the ship after the crew learned that Greenbark's family had been captured and [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]], and the next [[Standard day/Legends|day]], the pair joined the crew in planning a way to escape the area. When Jennir suggested that they try to persuade all the grounded vessels to take off at once to avoid any individual ship being immediately shot down, Snyffulnimatta interjected that the ''Valance'' would reveal the plan when contacted about it, to which Taanzer agreed. This led to a new plan in which Jennir instead stole a set of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|armor]] and then an [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|Alpha-3 ''Nimbus''-class V-wing starfighter]], which he used to fire on the grounded ''Valance''. As Jennir attacked, Schurk-Heren announced over an open [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] that the Imperials were attacking all grounded vessels, which led to every other vessel in the spaceport taking off to escape damage. Snyffulnimatta then successfully navigated the ''Uhumele'' off-world despite it coming under attack from V-wings, which Jennir, still in his stolen V-Wing, and [[Ko Vakier]], another member of the crew on the ''Uhumele''{{'s}} [[Turret/Legends|turret]], destroyed with their ship's weapons. Having escaped the immediate threat, the group agreed to travel to [[Orvax IV]], where Greenbark's family had been sold as slaves.<ref name="DT2" />

[[File:Sniffles_and_Janks.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Snyffulnimatta joined the rest of the ''Uhumele''{{'s}} crew dining on Esseles.]]
Once on Orvax, Snyffulnimatta remained on the ''Uhumele'' while Jennir, Greenbark, and Vakier, along with two other crew members—the [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] [[Mezgraf]] and the [[Tintinna/Legends|Tintinna]] [[Meekerdin-maa]]—searched the slave markets for Greenbark's [[Mesa Greenbark|wife]] and [[Resa Greenbark|daughter]].<ref name="DT3">[[Dark Times 3|''Dark Times'' 3]]</ref> The group returned without success, having found other Nosaurian women and children sold as slaves, but also having learned that Mesa Greenbark, Bomo's [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] trying to prevent their daughter Resa from being sold. Jennir, alone, then tracked down [[Orso Meeto]], the [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] slaver who had sold Resa, while Snyffulnimatta and the rest of the crew stayed put, keeping Greenbark from escaping and causing trouble due to his grief. Jennir then returned and informed the group that their next destination was the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], where a wealthy heir named [[Dezono Qua]] had Resa.<ref name="DT4">[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]]</ref> On Esseles, Snyffulnimatta joined the crew in enjoying a good quality meal in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Calamar]] while waiting for Jennir to gather information on Qua. Having learned that the slave owner lived in his family's isolated [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]]-like [[Qua family's villa|villa]], Jennir told the group that he didn't expect them to help him and Greenbark any further due to the danger involved, but the crew of the ''Uhumele'' refused to let the pair go alone.<ref name="DT5" />

Snyffulnimatta and Taanzer brought the ship to Qua's villa and dropped off the rest of the crew, before landing the ship securely. By the time they left the ship, Qua's [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] had been [[Assault on Dezono Qua's villa|destroyed or disabled]]. As they attempted to find the rest of the group, they heard the sound of Jennir, having learned that Qua had eaten Resa, shooting the wealthy man. The pair reached the crew just in time to hear Greenbark berate Jennir for taking a kill that was rightfully his and then revealing that Jennir was in fact a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Having learned this new information, Snyffulnimatta was among the crew members who, due to the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on all [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], wanted Jennir out of the crew. The Jedi Master obliged, remaining behind at Qua's home while the ''Uhumele'' left.<ref name="DT5" /> With Jennir gone, the crew traveled to the world of [[Pizkoss]] to pick up supplies, but while there, they were chased by Imperials. Snyffulnimatta and Taanzer managed to hastily get the ''Uhumele'' off-world, but one member of the crew, the [[Phindian/Legends|Phindian]] assistant [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] and [[Chef/Legends|cook]] [[Janks]], was captured.<ref name="DT6">[[Dark Times 6|''Dark Times'' 6]]</ref> While on Pizkoss, however, Captain Schurk-Heren had finalized a deal to sell the Uhumele's most valuable piece of cargo, which was unbeknownst to them the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifact named [[Dreypa's Oubliette]], to [[Haka Hai]], an old [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] colleague of Schurk-Heren's who had come to control a [[Haka Hai's gang|gang]] on the planet [[Mimban/Legends|Mimban]].<ref name="DT7">[[Dark Times 7|''Dark Times'' 7]]</ref>

===Death on Mimban===
{{Quote|Crys! Get down!|Snyffulnimatta takes a blaster bolt for Crys Taanzer|<ref name="DT8" />}}
[[File:Sniffles_Death.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Snyffulnimatta died saving Crys Taanzer.]]
The ''Uhumele'' retrieved their valuable cargo from an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] where it was hidden and traveled to Mimban to make their sale. Schurk-Heren, however, did not trust Hai and so instead brought a fake crate, rigged to explode if opened, with the plan to provide the location of the real crate once he'd been paid. The entire crew then transported the cargo through the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] of Mimban before arriving at [[Haka Hai's compound|Hai's compound]]. Once there, Schurk-Heren's mistrust of Hai proved correct, as after greeting the crew, the Ishi Tib ordered his men to aim their blasters at Schurk-Heren and his crew. Outnumbered three to one, the crew had no chance of fighting back, and Schurk-Heren agreed to simply leave his old friend the cargo in exchange for their lives. Hai refused, but before he could give the order to shoot, one of his men, a [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] named [[Lumbra]], revealed that he and a large group of Hai's men were betraying him and taking the cargo for themselves. A [[Skirmish on Mimban|battle]] quickly broke out between the two factions of Hai's men, and the ''Uhumele''{{'s}} crew saw their chance to escape.<ref name="DT7" /> As he attempted to flee for the exit, however, Snyffulnimatta saw one of Hai's men take aim at Taanzer. The Elomin flung himself between Taanzer and the shooter, taking the blaster shot in the back and sustaining a fatal wound. As Taanzer crouched over her dying savior, Snyffulnimatta complained that his one act of charity had cost him so much before dying in the pilot's [[Limb/Legends|arms]]. Shortly after his death, Hai's surviving men captured the rest of the ''Uhumele''{{'s}} crew, and the Ishi Tib had them put to work or thrown into the [[Prison/Legends|dungeons]] to be [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] for the location of the [[Oubliette (stasis casket)|Oubliette]], while Snyffulnimatta's body was tossed into the swamps.<ref name="DT8" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I knew this would happen. The first time I do anything for somebody else, and I get killed for it…|Snyffulnimatta's dying words|<ref name="DT8" />}}
[[File:Sniffles_on_the_Uhumele.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Snyffulnimatta was opposed to traveling with Dass Jennir after discovering he was a Jedi.]]
Lynaliskar K'ra Snyffulnimatta was a male Elomin with [[Color/Legends|crimson]] skin and white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="DT1" /> He was an able navigator and managed to work together with Crys Taanzer to get the ''Uhumele'' out of several difficult situations.<ref name="DT2" /><ref name="DT6" /> Snyffulnimatta greatly disliked the nickname of "Sniffles," which the rest of the crew gave to him, and complained whenever they used it.<ref name="DT1" /> Quite pessimistic in nature, he assumed the worst outcome would occur from many of the situations in which the ''Uhumele'' found itself, believing that if the ship tried to escape the spaceport in Cadgel Meadows, they would be betrayed by the crew of the ''Valance''.<ref name="DT2" /> Likewise, he was among the crew members of the ''Uhumele'' who wanted to be rid of Dass Jennir after discovering he was a Jedi, claiming that one bounty on the crew's head was enough and that Jennir would only make matters worse.<ref name="DT5" /> Despite his dour nature, Snyffulnimatta was reasonably fond of his crew mate Taanzer, shielding her from Jennir and Bomo Greenbark when they first encountered the pair as strangers.<ref name="DT1" /> He also chose to take a fatal blaster bolt for her; although he complained as he lay dying that his first act of charity had cost him his life, he claimed he had expected something like that would occur. After the Elomin's death, the crew of the ''Uhumele'' mourned Snyffulnimatta's loss, with Taanzer becoming particularly upset.<ref name="DT8" />

==Equipment==
Whilst a crew member of the ''Uhumele'' Snyffulnimatta generally wore an open green [[Clothing/Legends|jacket]] with yellow lining, bracelets, and green pants.<ref name="DT1" /> When dining on the planet Esseles, he wore blue [[Robe/Legends|robes]] instead.<ref name="DT5" /> In combat, he used a blaster.<ref name="DT1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lynaliskar K'ra Snyffulnimatta first appeared in [[Dark Times 1|''Dark Times'' 1]], the first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] and its [[Star Wars: Dark Times: The Path to Nowhere|''The Path to Nowhere'']] story arc.<ref name="DT1" /> The issue, released on [[November 8]], [[2006]],<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54075/star_wars_dark_times_2006_1|text=Star Wars: Dark Times (2006) #1}}</ref> was written by Mick Harrison,<ref name="DT1" /> a pen name of [[Randy Stradley]],<ref name="Insider 127">{{InsiderCite|127|Blaster}}</ref> and illustrated by [[Douglas Wheatley]].<ref name="DT1" />
